subject: mathematics
title: Class 4 students still count on their fingers for addition
query_text: Class 4 students still count on their fingers for addition

immediate_steps.title: Immediate Action
steps: Group students into pairs — one who counts on fingers, one who doesn't — for peer learning during daily practice
Introduce 'number anchor' cards: write 5 and 10 on the board as mental anchors, and practice making every number from these two anchors aloud as a class
Replace finger-counting with a daily 5-minute oral number-bond chant: '6 and 4 make 10, 7 and 3 make 10' — repetition builds automaticity

classroom_activity.title: Classroom Activity — Number Bond Pebble Game
classroom_activity.activity: Give each pair of students 10 small pebbles or seeds. One student hides some in one fist, rest in the other. Partner guesses the split. Example: 'I have 10 total, you see 6 in my open hand — how many are hidden?' Students say the answer aloud WITHOUT counting one by one. Rotate 5 rounds. This builds mental addition by anchoring to 10.
classroom_activity.materials: 10 pebbles, seeds, or small stones per pair — collected from schoolyard

inclusion_strategy.title: Inclusion Strategy
inclusion_strategy.strategy: For students who still need physical support, allow a drawn 10-frame on their slate (two rows of 5 boxes). They fill dots instead of using fingers — this transitions them from finger-counting to a structured visual tool, gradually building mental imagery without removing support abruptly.

reflection_prompt.title: Reflection
reflection_prompt.question: After trying the pebble game, which students moved from counting-on to recalling instantly — and what did those students do differently during the activity?

source_citation.title: Source
source_citation.reference: NIPUN Bharat Framework — Foundational Numeracy Goals, Number Operations & Mental Math strategies; NEP 2020 Section 2.1–2.2 — Foundational Literacy and Numeracy as urgent national priority; TaRL methodology (Pratham) — grouping by learning level for numeracy
